title: sovereign debt / investor composition / non bank investors | Research Paper | Who Holds Sovereign Debt and Why It Matters
summary: This BIS Working Paper analyzes sovereign debt holder composition across 95 countries (1991–2018), finding that private non-bank investors absorb disproportionately more sovereign debt supply than banks or official creditors. When debt supply increases, 69% flows to non-bank investors who hold only 46% of debt on average, while banks hold 28% but absorb only 20% of new issuance. Counterfactual analysis shows emerging market sovereigns face 6.7% cost increase per 10% debt increase normally, but 9% cost increase if non-bank investors are absent, highlighting EM vulnerability to non-bank investor flight.
